name: Dave Burns
description: American musician, trumpeter and music educator (1924–2009)
abstract: David Burns (March 24, 1924, Perth Amboy, New Jersey – April 5, 2009, Freeport, New York) was an American jazz trumpeter, flugelhornist, arranger, composer, and teacher. Burns began playing trumpet when he was nine years old, and heard bebop performances at Minton's Playhouse as a teenager, including Dizzy Gillespie.

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: we had a sextet for a small time a couple of years, Dave Burns, Bobby Hutcherson, Herman Wright, Candy Finch and Al and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: So I met Dizzy and Diz said to me and Dave Burns... 'come and try out for it.' So that's what we did when we were discharged -- I was discharged in 1946 and Dave was, too... And Dave Burns, trumpet.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: It was Tom McIntosh; Steve Davis, who was Trane’s bass player; Dave Burns, playing trumpet; Edgar Bateman was the drummer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: all of them were right in that band. It was a great band, and then I started going, playing with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: When I joined Dizzy’s band in ‘46... Dave Burns, Miles was in the band...
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: Dizzy, Dave Burns, John Brown, James Moody, Milt Jackson, Hank Jones… I don't know how they got all those people in Rudy Van Gelder’s studio
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
